subtasks.description: Evaluate the contractor's access request against data governance and access control policies. Assess whether temporary contractor status permits database backup downloads, check data classification restrictions (PII/confidential), verify approval requirements for bulk data exports.

Assess the security and compliance risk of approving this download. Evaluate data residency, encryption requirements, audit logging requirements, and contractor agreement terms. Recommend approval/denial with risk mitigations if applicable.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required: (1) policy_action to check governance rules against contractor access permissions and data classification — mid tier because contractor temporary status and bulk export policies require multi-clause reasoning; (2) cross_recon to reconcile policy outcome with security/compliance risk factors and generate a final recommendation — mid tier because it synthesizes policy constraints with operational risk signals. Not frontier-level because no complex multi-signal inference or anomaly detection needed; not small-level because contractor temporary status and data sensitivity require nuanced policy application.

output.reason: Contractor-dave's attempt to download the full production database backup (production_backup.tar.gz) is denied under all three applicable policies. POL-701 restricts database backup access exclusively to full-time employees holding the DBA role; contractor-dave holds neither status. POL-702 explicitly prohibits contractors from accessing raw data exports, limiting them to de-identified query results only — a full customer database backup contains raw PII and confidential data far exceeding this boundary. POL-703 further requires explicit project manager approval with a mandatory 7-day advance notice period before any contractor data access; no such approval is on record. All three policies independently constitute a hard block on this action. The recommended remediation path is: (1) immediately deny the download, (2) redirect contractor-dave to submit a scoped de-identified query request through the approved query interface, and (3) if bulk access is genuinely required for the project, escalate to the project manager to initiate the formal 7-day approval process with documented justification.
applied_constraints: Access limited to full-time DBA employees only (POL-701): contractor role disqualified
Raw data exports prohibited for contractors; only de-identified query results permitted (POL-702)
No documented project manager approval on file (POL-703)
Mandatory 7-day advance notice period not satisfied (POL-703)
Resource contains PII and confidential customer data — elevated data classification risk
Audit event must be logged: unauthorized download attempt by contractor-dave on production_backup.tar.gz
Security team and project manager must be notified of this access violation attempt
